jokes.text: Dance instructor's overly serious critique of Larry's choreography and shoes
jokes.setup: Professional dance instruction meets Larry's complete incompetence
jokes.punchline: Instructor's frustrated insistence on proper Capezio jazz oxfords for clearly hopeless student
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry fish-out-of-water setup. Cringe comes from professional taking amateur seriously.

jokes.text: Michael recognizes Larry from helping him move three years ago when Larry was soliciting strangers on the street
jokes.setup: Casual introduction between Larry and pianist
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Larry was desperately soliciting random strangers for moving help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - his desperation and lack of normal social connections exposed through chance encounter.

jokes.text: Michael proudly announces 'I hooked up with a model' about his blind girlfriend
jokes.setup: Michael introducing his girlfriend Rhonda
jokes.punchline: Blind man bragging about dating a 'model' when he can't see her
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for ongoing joke thread. The irony is perfect - blind man's pride about visual attributes.

jokes.text: Larry's President's Day analogy for Ben's belated birthday party
jokes.setup: Ben explaining why his birthday party is two weeks late
jokes.punchline: Larry comparing it to President's Day not being on the actual president's birthday
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic - finding absurd real-world parallel to call out social convention's arbitrariness.

jokes.text: Larry's blunt assessment: 'It's a total scam. He introduced her as a model. She's like completely bullshitting this guy 'cause he's blind.'
jokes.setup: Larry observing Michael and Rhonda's relationship
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutally honest analysis of the 'model' deception
jokes.explanation: Larry saying what everyone's thinking. Perfect character integration - his complete inability to let social nicety override truth.

jokes.text: Larry's awkward half-standup greeting for Richard's 'fake niece'
jokes.setup: Larry meeting Kim for the first time
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-aware commentary on his incomplete standing gesture
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy enhanced by Larry's self-consciousness about social etiquette.

jokes.text: Larry's transition from casual conversation to 'So Anna Nicole Smith, she's got uh... some pair of knockers on her, huh?' followed by awkward silence
jokes.setup: Normal conversation between Larry and aspiring actress Kim
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely inappropriate segue to discussing Anna Nicole Smith's breasts
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriateness. The abrupt topic change and subsequent silence create perfect cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Larry's shock at being the only person who took 'no gifts' literally
jokes.setup: Larry arrives at party without a gift while everyone else brought presents
jokes.punchline: 'You took that seriously?' reveals Larry's literal-mindedness vs social conventions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - his inability to decode social conventions creates genuine awkwardness.

jokes.text: Larry stuck holding a kebab skewer all night with nowhere to put it
jokes.setup: Larry gets handed a kebab skewer at the party
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy of being trapped with unwanted object that becomes recurring prop
jokes.explanation: Good physical setup for payoff later. Represents Larry's general discomfort in social situations.

jokes.text: Larry's blunt 'Eh' when Michael asks if he thinks Rhonda is beautiful
jokes.setup: Blind Michael asking Larry to validate his girlfriend's beauty
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely honest and deflating 'Eh' response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character integration. Larry's inability to lie even for kindness creates devastating moment.

jokes.text: Larry's assumption about blind people having better hearing: 'I thought you hear like a dog'
jokes.setup: Larry discussing advantages of blindness
jokes.punchline: Comparing blind person's hearing to a dog's
jokes.explanation: Larry's inappropriate but innocent curiosity about disability creates awkwardness through his poor word choice.

jokes.text: Larry pointing out advantages of being blind: 'That's one of the advantages, right? That and the good hearing.'
jokes.setup: Larry telling Michael that not being able to see Rhonda is an advantage
jokes.punchline: Larry casually listing blindness benefits like it's a job perk
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriateness - treating disability as having benefits in dating. His tone-deaf social skills on full display.

jokes.text: Susie's shirt reveal and Larry's honest reaction: 'Not quite my cup of tea'
jokes.setup: Susie excitedly showing off her shirt designs
jokes.punchline: Larry's diplomatic but clearly unimpressed response
jokes.explanation: Good setup for escalating conflict. Larry tries diplomacy but fails to hide his true feelings.

jokes.text: Susie's explosive response: 'Fuck you and fuck your tea. Whoever said you had taste, Mr. Hushpuppy-rumpled-suit look?'
jokes.setup: Larry's mild criticism of Susie's shirts
jokes.punchline: Susie's disproportionate rage and specific insult about his fashion sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and character writing. 'Hushpuppy-rumpled-suit look' is brilliantly specific insult.

jokes.text: Larry's rant about birthday party statute of limitations
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his objection to Ben's delayed birthday party
jokes.punchline: Detailed analysis of acceptable timeframe for belated celebrations with legal terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic taken to absurd extreme. The 'statute of limitations' framing is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Larry's kebab and scrotum connection: 'Stick yourself in the scrotum? Then what do you got? Scrotum kebab.'
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about kebab skewers
jokes.punchline: Logical progression from pocket storage to accidental injury to 'scrotum kebab'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of mundane complaint to absurd conclusion. 'Scrotum kebab' is peak Larry logic and incredibly quotable.

jokes.text: Larry hearing 'I love tits' in telephone game instead of 'I love pigs'
jokes.setup: Larry playing telephone with children at birthday party
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate interpretation/mishearing leads to public accusation
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy. Larry's mind in the gutter creates scandal at children's party. Great setup for later accusations.

jokes.text: Larry refusing to sing Happy Birthday: 'I hate the song. I don't sing that.'
jokes.setup: Ben asking why Larry didn't participate in birthday song
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact hatred of universally accepted song
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry contrarianism. His principled stand against innocent tradition is beautifully character-specific.

jokes.text: Larry refusing to sing Happy Birthday: 'I hate the song. I don't sing that.'
jokes.setup: Everyone singing Happy Birthday to Ben
jokes.punchline: Larry's principled stand against the song
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Larry - turning universal ritual into personal rebellion

jokes.text: Larry accidentally stabbing Ben in the eye with the kebab skewer during golf swing demonstration
jokes.setup: Larry demonstrating golf tip using skewer as club
jokes.punchline: Inevitable physical comedy payoff as skewer becomes weapon
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy payoff. The skewer Larry complained about all night finally causes the disaster he predicted.

jokes.text: Larry's inability to relate to blind Michael: 'It's hard to talk to a blind guy. You have no references.'
jokes.setup: Larry trying to reference Flash comic book character
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt assessment of communication difficulties with blind people
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry inappropriate honesty. His frustration with having to adjust communication style is perfectly selfish.

jokes.text: Larry's Brad Pitt comparison: 'I'd like to tell my wife I look like Brad Pitt, but unfortunately she can see.'
jokes.setup: Michael's relationship issues with Rhonda misrepresenting herself
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-deprecating analogy about visual deception
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware joke. Perfect economy and structure. Larry's self-deprecation makes the point about deception perfectly.

jokes.text: Ben's complaint about Larry not shaking hands because of snot speckles
jokes.setup: Ben cataloging Larry's social failures
jokes.punchline: Detailed argument about dry vs. wet sneezes and snot speckle visibility
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of petty grievance. Larry's paranoia about hygiene vs. social convention creates perfect conflict.

jokes.text: Kim showing off her new breast implants to Larry
jokes.setup: Kim visiting Larry to show her headshots
jokes.punchline: Surprise reveal and insistence that Larry touch her new breasts
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff. The awkwardness of forced intimate contact creates classic Larry predicament.

jokes.text: Cheryl's reaction to Larry touching Kim's breasts: 'Happy anniversary. Because that was your 10th anniversary gift right there.'
jokes.setup: Larry trying to explain innocent breast touching incident
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's sarcastic declaration that touching breasts equals their anniversary celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect spouse response to Larry's situation. Deadpan delivery makes the accusation funnier than anger would.

jokes.text: Cheryl calling the breast touching Larry's '10th anniversary gift'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the innocent nature of the breast touching
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's cutting reframe as infidelity
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback that recontextualizes the innocent encounter

jokes.text: Larry's titmouse/mouse confusion leading to Cheryl's 'You are obsessed with tits' accusation
jokes.setup: Larry spotting a bird and calling it a titmouse
jokes.punchline: Cheryl connecting it to pattern of breast-related incidents
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that ties together multiple plot threads. Larry's innocent bird identification becomes damning evidence.

jokes.text: Larry calling a bird a 'titmouse' and Cheryl's reaction about his tit obsession
jokes.setup: Larry spots a bird and names it
jokes.punchline: Cheryl connecting it to his supposed breast obsession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant triple callback - bird name triggers wife's paranoia about tit obsession

jokes.text: Larry's defense about the telephone game: 'The kid said, "I love tits." That's what he said to me.'
jokes.setup: Cheryl listing Larry's inappropriate behavior with children
jokes.punchline: Larry's insistence that child actually said inappropriate thing
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows how Larry's mishearing has become ammunition against him in marital fight.

jokes.text: Larry's telephone game defense escalation
jokes.setup: Cheryl listing Larry's breast-related incidents
jokes.punchline: Larry's increasingly desperate defense of the telephone mishearing
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows how all the incidents compound Larry's guilt

jokes.text: Michael's callous 'You need to be with a good-looking woman? Who do you think you are?' after breaking up with Rhonda because of Larry's honesty
jokes.setup: Michael asking Larry for help after breaking up with girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Larry's shock at Michael's shallow reasoning despite being the one who pointed out Rhonda wasn't attractive
jokes.explanation: Good consequence comedy. Larry's honesty creates exactly the result he wanted but he's shocked by Michael's shallowness.

jokes.text: Larry buying Susie's shirt to make amends with Ben
jokes.setup: Larry needing to apologize to Ben with a gift
jokes.punchline: Having to buy the shirt he insulted while pretending he loves it
jokes.explanation: Good callback resolution. Larry forced to eat his words literally by purchasing what he criticized.

jokes.text: Larry buying Susie's shirt as a gift for Ben
jokes.setup: Larry needs to buy Ben a gift and remembers the shirt incident
jokes.punchline: His plan to buy the shirt he criticized for the person who supposedly loved it
jokes.explanation: Good setup for resolution that uses Larry's social manipulation skills

jokes.text: Larry's complaint about kebab sticks: 'I saw five people holding sticks. I did. I was checking.'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his vindication about party food problems
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Larry was actively surveying party to prove his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry behavior - conducting informal social science to prove his petty point. The fact that he was 'checking' is beautifully obsessive.

jokes.text: Larry's elaborate justification for wanting the shirt: 'It's like you go to a movie and it kind of stays with you'
jokes.setup: Larry pretending he genuinely wants to buy Susie's shirt
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic explanation that reveals his obvious desperation to make amends
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's bad lying skills. The elaborate metaphor makes his deception obvious.

jokes.text: Ben's enthusiasm for the shirt Larry hated: 'I love these' / 'She told me how much you liked them'
jokes.setup: Larry giving Ben the shirt as an apology gift
jokes.punchline: Ben's genuine love for the shirt Larry despised, plus revelation that Susie lied about Ben liking them
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic resolution. Larry's forced gift choice turns out to be genuinely appreciated, revealing Susie's manipulation.

jokes.text: Larry's confusion about stage directions: 'Is that my left, or "if I'm sitting in the audience" my left?'
jokes.setup: Director giving Larry basic stage direction
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete confusion about fundamental theater concept
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's fish-out-of-water status in theater world. Mild but consistent with his incompetence.
